commands.command: python -m py_compile research audit helper scripts
commands.summary: helper scripts compiled; generated pycache removed

findings: Research workflow now includes a generic Research Reasoning Atlas stage with branch tree, debate matrix, alternative explanations, confounder ledger, model-lens selection, expert stance map, causal-chain questions, counterfactual traces, conclusion tournaments, and future falsifiers.

SourceGuard now plans branch-aware discovery, disconfirming-source searches, source-lineage checks, expert-stance sources, model sources, and branch-distinguishing evidence without hard-coding any single topic domain.
TraceGuard now preserves competing storylines, causal-chain weak links, counterfactual uncertainty, confounders, and red-team storylines before LogicGuard handoff.

LogicGuard now requires central conclusions to survive a conclusion tournament with steelman opposition, live alternatives, model-lens warrants, expert-role boundaries, robustness tests, and allowed wording.
Installed Codex skill copies were synchronized from edited local source skill directories and hash-verified.
counterexamples: FlowGuard duplicate-boundary fixture remains blocked to prevent a new all-in-one research engine from replacing SourceGuard, TraceGuard, or LogicGuard ownership.
FlowGuard stale-validation fixture remains blocked to prevent completion claims after installed-skill edits without current validation.
Research helper bad fixtures fail as expected for undefined source markers and missing source-role coverage.
friction_points: An initial installed-skill hash check exposed that the first nested PowerShell copy attempt had not synchronized all files; direct per-file PowerShell copy fixed the mismatch and full parity passed.
quick_validate.py requires the skill directory argument; running without it returns usage text.
skipped_steps: No remote push, release publication, or git commit was performed.
No standalone FlowGuard skill was changed; FlowGuard remains process/freshness/closure governance, not the research reasoning engine.
risk_evidence_summary: The upgrade is intentionally generic: domain details must stay in selected model lenses, examples, or future lens packs, not core skill prompts.

Branch volume alone is not treated as quality; final quality gates require branch relevance, disconfirming evidence, source lineage, competing storylines, conclusion tournament status, and reader-facing wording boundaries.
next_actions: Archive the OpenSpec change after user approval if this implementation should become the accepted spec baseline.

task_summary: Strengthen layered source, trace, argument, and artifact-quality contracts across ResearchWorkflow, SourceGuard, TraceGuard, and LogicGuard skills
trigger_reason: User requested OpenSpec and FlowGuard governed upgrade after comparing local research output with ChatGPT Deep Research and then explicitly requested deeper TraceGuard and LogicGuard hierarchy down to sections, paragraphs, and local artifact units
skill_decision: use_direct_flowguard_skill: existing_model_preflight plus development_process_flow; companion OpenSpec change strengthen-layered-research-evidence-contract

package 0.40.4
project audit passed
change valid
all four installed skills valid

commands.command: python -m py_compile research ledger helper scripts
commands.summary: source portfolio, key-claim ledger, and semantic-fit helpers compile
known-good fixtures passed; known-bad fixtures failed for missing portfolio classes, missing key-claim provenance, and semantic-fit mismatches
sourceguard --help, traceguard --help, and logicguard run script --help passed
source portfolio, key-claim ledger, semantic source-fit, layered TraceGuard, hierarchical LogicGuard, genre-adaptive review, and AI data-center mismatch patterns found
valid layered evidence route passed; duplicate-boundary all-in-one engine fixture blocked as expected
valid layered evidence lifecycle passed; stale-validation fixture blocked as expected

findings: Research workflow now requires source portfolio planning, key-claim/key-number ledgers, transmission/effect chains, semantic source-fit checks, hierarchical Guard modeling, and genre-adaptive final artifact review before strong closure claims.

SourceGuard guidance now treats broad bibliography as insufficient and requires source-class coverage, source-lineage independence, key-number provenance, and bridge evidence for scope/time/effect-layer jumps.
TraceGuard guidance now requires layered lead modeling and effect-chain/scope-transfer status before handing impact, price, market, policy, adoption, execution, or outcome claims to LogicGuard.

LogicGuard guidance now adapts to the requested artifact genre and models high-importance units at natural hierarchy, such as document-section-paragraph or deck-section-slide, instead of judging all outputs as fixed reports.
FlowGuard remained lifecycle/process governance only; no FlowGuard report-writing behavior was changed.
counterexamples: The duplicate all-in-one research engine model remains blocked by ExistingModelPreflight.
The stale lifecycle model remains blocked when validation evidence predates later skill edits.
Helper bad fixtures fail when regional/forecast/announcement/planning evidence is used to support national/observed/outcome/retail wording without bridge evidence.
skipped_steps: No remote push or release publication was performed.
FlowGuard's own installed skill behavior was not modified.
risk_evidence_summary: The contract improves structure and evidence discipline but helper scripts are structural warnings, not factual truth engines; LogicGuard still owns exact wording and semantic support judgment.
Deeper hierarchy should be used for substantive source-backed artifacts, while small quick answers may downgrade to lightweight leads or hypotheses.
next_actions: Commit intended source repository changes and archive the OpenSpec change after implementation is accepted if desired.
